Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly identifies Rhg1-GmAAT as Glyma.18G022400.

Applied_Species.reason: The gene was functionally tested in soybean throughout the Results, including transgenic soybean, NILs, PI88788, and Williams 82 comparisons.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The Experimental Details explicitly state soybean as Glycine max.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Results and methods directly use cultivar Tianlong 1, PI88788, Williams 82, and near-isogenic lines NIL-R/NIL-S.

Source_Species.reason: The cloned gene is soybean Rhg1-GmAAT.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Soybean is explicitly given as Glycine max in Experimental Details.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state the full-length coding sequence and promoter region used for constructs were amplified from soybean Williams 82 cDNA/genomic DNA.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measure changes in free glutamic acid in roots/leaves/phloem and jasmonic acid plus JA-Ile in roots of Rhg1-GmAAT-OX plants.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Glutamic acid is an amino acid, while JA and JA-Ile are jasmonates/oxylipin phytohormones; this classification is consistent with the paper's context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper discusses glutamate in transport/homeostasis/signalling and presents evidence that JA pathway activation contributes to SCN resistance context in Rhg1-containing germplasm.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Root glutamate, JA, and JA-Ile were measured and increased in roots; leaf glutamate and leaf phloem exudate amino acids were also measured, including elevated Glu in leaf phloem exudates.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Results state Rhg1-GmAAT encodes a 436-aa protein containing one amino acid transporter domain from aa 22 to 426 and shares 58.72%/58.31% identity with AtAVT6C/AtAVT6D.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results state the fusion protein was observed in the plasma membrane and nucleus, and might also exist in the cytoplasm.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: qRT-PCR showed ubiquitous expression with highest levels in roots, stems and cotyledons; promoter-GUS showed vascular expression, with strong signal in stele and enriched signal in vascular tissues/pericycle of hairy roots.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The expression and promoter analyses were performed on the soybean gene itself; the same ubiquitous/vascular pattern applies to the source soybean gene.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support enhanced shoot-to-root glutamate transport, elevated root glutamate, activation of JA-related genes and JA/JA-Ile accumulation, reduced SCN resistance when JA biosynthesis is inhibited in PI88788, and no SCN resistance from Rhg1-GmAAT overexpression alone.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly described and used in Results/Methods: overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean, NIL comparison, promoter-GUS, GFP localization, Glu/MSX assays, HPLC-MS/MS amino acid profiling, RNA-seq, qRT-PCR, JA/JA-Ile quantification, and SCN assay with nPG.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods describe 35S-driven overexpression/localization constructs and native Rhg1-GmAAT promoter fused to GUS.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper clearly reports RNA-seq and metabolite profiling/targeted HPLC-MS/MS, but no public accession is provided; marking accession as NA is consistent with absence of an accession in the paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments are all explicitly used: exogenous glutamate, MSX, n-propyl gallate, and SCN infection.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show overexpression alone did not confer SCN resistance and metabolite profiling found many compounds suppressed; amino acid profiling also showed decreases in several amino acids.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports that Rhg1-GmAAT is one component of Rhg1 and that overexpression alone is insufficient for SCN resistance, but the forward-looking engineering claim that it 'may be leveraged to modulate root glutamate/JA homeostasis' is not explicitly stated or tested as an application.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The Summary explicitly says direct glutamate transport was not demonstrated, but multiple lines of evidence support impacts on glutamate transport/homeostasis; the paper also states three Rhg1 genes are required together for resistance.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results: predicted transporter with vascular expression and plasma membrane/nuclear localization; specific Glu/MSX tolerance and sensor gene induction; enhanced shoot-to-root Glu transport with root increase and leaf decrease; JA pathway activation and higher JA/JA-Ile; reduced resistance upon JA inhibition in PI88788; and lack of resistance from Rhg1-GmAAT alone.
